efficacyPairs.questionText: Choose one word from each group that are closest in meaning.
efficacyPairs.lessonTeachSummary: Think about the word **"destroy"** — it means to completely wreck or ruin something. Now imagine someone doing the action. Does **"demolish"** show the EXACT same picture?
lessonLearningGoal: How to find synonyms for action words (verbs) — imagine the action like a video in your head!
Why picturing the action helps you find the match every time

efficacyPairs.questionText: Choose one word from each group that are closest in meaning.
efficacyPairs.lessonTeachSummary: The word **"purchase"** is just a fancy way of saying **"buy"**. Loads of 11+ synonym questions pair a **formal word** with its **everyday twin** — they mean exactly the same thing. Once you spot the
lessonLearningGoal: How to recognise that formal and informal words can be synonyms — every posh word has a simple twin hiding in plain sight!
Why the 11+ loves pairing a fancy word with a simple one (and how to crack it every time)

efficacyPairs.questionText: Choose one word from each group that are closest in meaning.
efficacyPairs.lessonTeachSummary: The word **"purchase"** is just a fancy way of saying **"buy"**. Loads of 11+ synonym questions pair a **formal word** with its **everyday twin** — they mean exactly the same thing. Once you spot the
lessonLearningGoal: How to recognise that formal and informal words can be synonyms — every posh word has a simple twin hiding in plain sight!

efficacyPairs.questionText: Choose one word from each group that are closest in meaning.
efficacyPairs.lessonTeachSummary: Here's a brilliant trick you can use anywhere! Pop **"happy"** and **"glad"** into the same sentence — if the meaning stays **exactly** the same, they're synonyms. Easy!
lessonLearningGoal: How to spot everyday synonym (words that mean the same thing) pairs — you already know more of these than you realise!
How to use the swap test — pop a word into a sentence, swap it out, and if the meaning stays the same, you've found a synonym. This trick is golden!

efficacyPairs.questionText: Choose one word from each group that are closest in meaning.

How to use the swap test — pop a word into a sentence, swap it out, and if the meaning stays the same, you've found a synonym. This trick is golden!

efficacyPairs.questionText: Choose one word from each group that are closest in meaning.
efficacyPairs.lessonTeachSummary: The word **"scared"** is a medium-level emotion. Its synonym must be at the **same strength** — think of it like a volume dial:
lessonLearningGoal: How to match emotion words at the same strength level — think of emotions like a volume dial!
How to tell the difference between similar emotions (angry vs annoyed vs furious) — this is trickier than it sounds!

efficacyPairs.questionText: Choose one word from each group that are closest in meaning.
efficacyPairs.lessonTeachSummary: The word **"purchase"** is just a fancy way of saying **"buy"**. Loads of 11+ synonym questions pair a **formal word** with its **everyday twin** — they mean exactly the same thing. Once you spot the

efficacyPairs.questionText: Choose one word from each group that are closest in meaning.
efficacyPairs.lessonTeachSummary: **"enormous"** describes the a building's quality. A describing-word synonym must describe the **same quality** — size with size, speed with speed, age with age.
lessonLearningGoal: How to find synonyms for describing words (adjectives) — what quality is being described?
Why checking what is being described helps find the match every time

efficacyPairs.questionText: Choose one word from each group that are closest in meaning.

efficacyPairs.questionText: Choose one word from each group that are closest in meaning.
efficacyPairs.lessonTeachSummary: **"diligent"** means working hard and carefully. When you see a hard word, **cross out options you KNOW are wrong** — this narrows it down fast.
lessonLearningGoal: How to recognise harder synonym pairs that appear in the 11+ — these are the ones that earn top marks!
How to use elimination when you're not sure of a word (this strategy is a lifesaver)

lessonLearningGoal: How to find synonyms for describing words (adjectives) — what quality is being described?
Why checking what is being described helps find the match every time

efficacyPairs.questionText: Choose one word from each group that are closest in meaning.
efficacyPairs.lessonTeachSummary: Think about the word **"destroy"** — it means to completely wreck or ruin something. Now imagine someone doing the action. Does **"demolish"** show the EXACT same picture?
lessonLearningGoal: How to find synonyms for action words (verbs) — imagine the action like a video in your head!
Why picturing the action helps you find the match every time

efficacyPairs.questionText: Choose one word from each group that are closest in meaning.
efficacyPairs.lessonTeachSummary: **"diligent"** means working hard and carefully. When you see a hard word, **cross out options you KNOW are wrong** — this narrows it down fast.
lessonLearningGoal: How to recognise harder synonym pairs that appear in the 11+ — these are the ones that earn top marks!
How to use elimination when you're not sure of a word (this strategy is a lifesaver)

efficacyPairs.questionText: Choose one word from each group that are closest in meaning.
efficacyPairs.lessonTeachSummary: Here's a brilliant trick you can use anywhere! Pop **"happy"** and **"glad"** into the same sentence — if the meaning stays **exactly** the same, they're synonyms. Easy!
lessonLearningGoal: How to spot everyday synonym (words that mean the same thing) pairs — you already know more of these than you realise!
How to use the swap test — pop a word into a sentence, swap it out, and if the meaning stays the same, you've found a synonym. This trick is golden!
